reviews.review_msg: The answer keys to the weekly assignments are consistently incorrect. Not too good when studying for an exam using these questions.
Never again.

-Read of slides every lecture that he posted (doesn't really teach)
-the average for my midterm was 40%
-If you didn't take any macro prior to this course it's rough
just don't. Just no...

Will take points off of an exam if you add too much/feels it is irrelevant. Admits his tests are too/sometimes can't even solve his own questions. Has VERY low averages for tests so he has to curve very high to be on par with other 105 classes. READ EVERYTHING on the slides,

He curved the class at C+, when all the other professors do at B. Especially for a upper division class. 
Only created an accnt here to prevent people to take class with him. You are better off not attending class as he will only confuse you, which kinda sucks since im paying a fortune as I am an international student. STUDY ON YOUR OWN.

Respectfully, take this course with another teacher. John has no clue how to teach. If you have no choice but to take this course, read the textbook like it's the Bible; consistently and religiously. That textbook is the only thing saving me. Also, the final is 50% of the grade, which I'm taking in less than an hour. Pray.

The course material itself is not to difficult, however I found the way he taught concepts to be confusing. It felt sort of like the class was taught out of order. If you take this course make sure you study concepts on your own outside of his lectures. His midterm was fair but difficult as understanding the concepts is not enough to do well.

I am at a loss for words, this man needs to be stopped. John Knowles can't keep getting away with this. If you see this rating, never take Econ 105 with this man. Run. Run far far away until you have reached a safe place. From his lectures to his tutorial quizzes to midterm and now the final, it has been a horrific,awful,disintegrating experience.

Don't let any other classes fool you. John Knowles 105 class is a nightmare. The lecture slides are no help and the man refuses to use a microphone but its okay he barley talks about the class topic. I learned more economics in high school. But the last straw was when he kept getting his own practice final wrong. Shame on SFU for letting him teach.

By far the least competent professor at the entirety of SFU. There is no point in going to his lectures as he reads everything he teaches off of the slides or from the textbook verbatim. He often doesn't know how to answer the questions he poses and literally provides nothing of substance, I stopped attending and found my grade improved.

There are professors that want to see you do well and then there profs like Knowels. There's no cohesion to his ECON 105 course, it feels like a bunch of random topics thrown together at random. To add to it, he cannot explain those topics very well. His classes are also indescribably boring--every single one.
cheeks. dont take

Lectures were based off his slides. He did NOT know his own material and often backtracked on everything he said. Mind numbingly boring and a terrible teacher overall. One time a question popped up on the slides and he said "I actually don't know how to solve this, but it'll probably be on the mid-term". He overcomplicates easy concepts.

Stubborn and fixated on extremely dated takes on teaching, he ambles through his lectures almost completely incompetently. His first comment to the class was essentially "Professors arent supposed to be good at teaching, high school teachers are better at that". Overall, extremely unserious about his job and difficult in his ways. Not recommended!
Highly reccomend this course to econ major students. Pretty straightforward. Have weekly quizes and readings but east to keep up. Just read the slides and go through review questions for exams.

Lectures were almost purely off of his poorly made slides, point by point. Barely knew his own material and often time had to stop himself to figure out what he was supposed to be saying. Tutorial quizzes were largely a guessing game in regards to the material covered. Gave review material without answers or weeks late at best.Testing was not clear
Don't take this class
Super easy course and chill professor - don't miss the tutorials!

The person who below most likely is lying about their grade. The class is graded on a midterm and final (90% in total). John's lectures are very heavy with material, he avoids calculus as much as possible. He gives you old exams to study and they're very similar in nature. Overall, he's not as bad as people say and the class is relatively easy.

My midterm and final were both well above average, and his class average was a B+, but when I asked him why I got a B, he gave me the response that his grade was in the B range, which was surprising. By the way for students who did not get good grades in the midterm, he deferred them to the end of the period, which is not a fair approach. Stay away

John's lectures are pretty dry but his examinations are more than fair! You really just need to read the textbook and you're good to go. His review questions are relevant and his teaching is clear. Would reccommend

I had him for econ 310 and 305 and I can say he is really good. I Highley recommend taking classes with him. Make sure to go over the textbook question and understand his lectures prior to the exam. He explains concepts very clearly. if you have an interest in Monetary economics, then there is no one better to assist you with your questions.
Too hard

He is a professor you would like to go for a beer but won't like his teaching. Covers a lot of stuff. You have to work really hard and read the lecture notes thoroughly. Use book if necessary but his lecture notes are sufficient. Tough grader as he has negative marking. Makes it look easy but actually his exams are tricky and wording is vague
awesome

John he is a good professor, he is kind and always willing to help. When you go through all his lecture notes and understand what he is trying to explain you will find the examinations are not hard. Good professor that I will recommend to others.
His class is just a pain. Don't take ECON305 from him. Notes don't help and DON'T expect good marks on the exams without the text book. Literally you have to study all by yourself.

John is out to lunch. He does not explain concepts very well, and does not keep track of what he's "taught" you. His exam and assignment questions are worded in a way that is difficult to understand. Do not take this course with John. I was fortunate that I had an incredible TA.

you will need to read the text book and his lecture notes do not help, they are just  questions form the book without answer. The prof is fine but his exams are difficult. DO NOT skip his lecture! he gives pop quiz almost every class.
Such a bad prof, even the lecture is wrong. The exam makes no sense at all. Wont take his course again.
Horrible handwriting, very very terrible. Can't read his handwriting even though seating in the first row. So many mistake when he writes down the notes.

Not a good Prof.  Covers alot of material, more that most 305 classes.  Expect lecture notes to be incomplete and his test to be nothing like assigned homework questions.  We had a guest prof come in when Prof. Knowles couldnt make it and even he was criticizing his notes.  Would not recommend, while upper div econ is never easy this class was HARD

Get to know the textbook really well because he covers a lot of material really fast in lectures.  Expect the lecture notes to be riddled with errors and for the answer keys to problem sets to be wrong.  Just do a lot of independent learning and you should be fine.

If I say this is an easy A i am lying but you really have to read and understand this course. HAVE TO READ BOTH lecture sides and textbooks very very very nice Prof, he does not give you a free A you have to work for it and he is always open for questions during class and office hours.
